title: 10 best herbal teas for ringworm

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with ringworm because it possesses anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties that effectively combat fungal infections. The gel from the aloe vera plant has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including ringworm. When consumed as a tea, the soothing properties of aloe can help reduce redness, itching, and inflammation associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications. Additionally, aloe's antifungal properties can help eliminate the fungal infection at its source, leading to a more complete recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antifungal properties: The antimicrobial compounds in aloe vera, including salicylic acid and saponins, inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection spreading.

Softering and protective properties: Aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects help calm irritated skin, reduce scratching, and create a protective barrier against further fungal infection and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain antibacterial and antifungal properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm, making them effective in treating skin conditions.
Leaves: The gel inside aloe leaves has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce itching, redness, and inflammation associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and trim them into small pieces for infusion.
Combine one cup of water with two tablespoons of trimmed aloe vera in a pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids before refrigerating or freezing it.
Drink two cups of the cooled tea twice daily for at least 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this condition. The tea contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol and alpha-pinene, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote healing. When consumed as a tea or applied topically, these compounds can help soothe symptoms like itching and inflammation, while also working to eliminate the underlying infection and promote healthy skin regrowth.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Certain herbal teas like tea tree oil-infused tea or tea made from herbs like goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) and myrrh (Commiphora molmol) have antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Teas like calendula (Calendula officinalis) and plantain (Plantago major) may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery.

Antimicrobial properties: Teas made from herbs like garlic (Allium sativum), ginger (Zingiber officinale), and chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, tea tree roots have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: The barks of the tea tree contain oils with potent antimicrobial activities, making them effective against ringworm-causing fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a healthy plant for maximum potency.
Rinse 2 teaspoons of harvested leaves with filtered water to remove any impurities.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot and add the rinsed leaves for 10-15 minutes steeping time.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Let the cooled tea sit for at least 30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as calendine and triterpenoids, work to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, ultimately reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants help to soothe and calm affected skin, promoting faster healing and resolution of symptoms. By drinking pot marigold tea regularly, individuals can potentially alleviate the itching, redness, and scaling associated with ringworm, making it an effective natural remedy for this common skin condition.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Pot marigold tea contains antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids that inhibit fungal growth, effectively combating the fungus that causes ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as luteolin, can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's saponins and flavonoids promote collagen synthesis, improve wound hydration, and enhance tissue repair, which aids in the healing of skin lesions caused by ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and other minerals, roots help to promote skin healing and reduce inflammation associated with ringworm.
Seeds: Containing antifungal compounds, seeds inhibit fungal growth and combat ringworm infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula officinalis flowers in late morning or early afternoon when they are in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for later use.
Mix one teaspoon of dried calendula officinalis flowers with boiling water to create a soothing tea infusion that calms ringworm symptoms.
Steep the herbal mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavonoids and sesquiterpenes to release their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any remaining plant material and enjoy the warm, slightly bitter flavor.
Drink the calendula tea three times a day for 7-10 days to help alleviate ringworm symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with ringworm because of its unique composition of compounds that have antifungal properties. The tea's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal cells, including those responsible for ringworm infections. By drinking maidenhair tree tea regularly, individuals can help to boost their body's natural defenses against fungal infections, reducing the severity and duration of ringworm symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Ginkgo biloba tea contains antifungal compounds such as terpenoids and flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause ringworm, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate the healing process and prevent complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that help to reduce fungal infections and promote wound healing.
Rhyzomes: Rich in antimicrobial properties that combat ringworm-causing fungi and prevent further infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall, selecting only fresh and healthy specimens.
Clean the harvested leaves by rinsing them thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep one tablespoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or two tablespoons of fresh leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discarding the solids and retaining only the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool before applying it topically to the affected area using a clean cotton swab or compress three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and luteolin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause ringworm infections. Additionally, English lavender has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm. Drinking English lavender tea may also help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which exhibit antimicrobial activity against fungi, bacteria, and viruses, including those that cause ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenolic compounds in lavender tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.

Antifungal properties: Lavender tea's antifungal properties are particularly effective against Candida spp., a common cause of fungal infections like ringworm, and can help prevent the spread of the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain antibacterial and antifungal compounds that help combat ringworm, making them a valuable component in medicinal teas.
Seeds: The seeds of the English lavender plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the affected area and reduce inflammation associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers, gather 2 tablespoons of dried or 3-4 teaspoons of fresh petals for every cup of boiling water.
Measure one cup of water and bring it to a rolling boil in a saucepan, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Add the measured lavender petals to the hot water, cover the saucepan with a lid, and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, consuming 1/2 cup twice daily for best results in reducing ringworm symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic teas helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which allow it to effectively combat fungal infections such as ringworm. The active compound allicin in garlic tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of garlic help to boost the body's natural defenses, allowing for a faster recovery from ringworm symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to treat ringworm without harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Garlic's antimicrobial compounds, such as allicin and ajoene, can inhibit fungal growth and reduce symptoms of ringworm infection, including itching, redness, and scaling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and sulfur-containing compounds in garlic tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling, itching, and discomfort associated with ringworm infection.

Antibacterial activity: Garlic's antibacterial properties can also contribute to its effectiveness against ringworm, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that may be present on the skin or in the affected area, and preventing secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots contain a high concentration of sulfur compounds, which have been shown to have antifungal properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem of the garlic plant, and it contains a high amount of allicin, a compound that has potent antimicrobial properties, making it useful for creating medicinal teas to combat ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs, peel them, and chop into small pieces for a total of two tablespoons.
Combine the chopped garlic with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or large mug.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes before straining the tea through a fine-mesh sieve.
Add one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and let it steep for an additional five minutes before serving.
Strain the tea again, pour into a cup, and consume immediately while warm or slightly cooled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with ringworm because it contains berberine, a compound that has potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. When consumed as a tea, berberine is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the affected area, where it inhibits the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause ringworm. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting a faster healing process and alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.

Berbamine: Exhibits potent antiviral and antibacterial activity, which assists in fighting off the causative agent of ringworm (Trichophyton spp.) and other microorganisms that contribute to the infection.

Amino Acids: Goldenseal contains a range of amino acids, including berberine-bound amino acids, which help to stimulate the immune system and promote its natural response to fungal infections like ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural antifungal agent that helps combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: Contains berbamine and columbamine, two alkaloids with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder from a reputable source.
Combine the powder with eight ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized teapot.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes, then strain and discard the solids.
Add two tablespoons of honey to sweeten and enhance the tea's antibacterial properties.
Drink the tea twice daily, one hour before meals, for four to six weeks to help alleviate ringworm symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which work to combat the fungus that causes the infection. The tea's compounds, such as fatty acids and sterols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause ringworm. Additionally, saw palmetto has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with ringworm. By drinking saw palmetto teas regularly, individuals can support their body's natural defenses against fungal infections and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions like prostatitis or enlarged prostate.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in saw palmetto tea may protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which could contribute to its potential benefits for urinary tract health and prostate issues.

Estrogen-balancing: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT) may help regulate hormone levels and alleviate symptoms related to benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) or hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to combat fungal infections, making them effective against ringworm.
Fruits: Saw palmetto fruits contain fatty acids and phytosterols that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and heal infected skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of dried saw palmetto berries from the serenoa repens plant.
Combine the harvested saw palmetto berries with four cups of boiling water in a large saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-25 minutes allowing the mixture to steep and extract.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or container.
Allow the tea to cool before serving and consuming as needed throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which allow it to combat fungal infections that cause the condition. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work together to inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce inflammation in the affected area. Additionally, stinging nettle's antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial overgrowth, promoting a healthy environment for healing and reducing the risk of further infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm.
Antifungal activity: The tea's antifungal properties, particularly due to the presence of beta-sitosterol, can help combat fungal infections such as ringworm by inhibiting their growth and spread.

Antioxidant activity: Stinging nettle tea is rich in antioxidants like vitamin C and E, which help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, making it easier for the body to fight off infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing and healing skin infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of stinging nettle have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including fungal infections like ringworm, due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves in early morning or evening when dew is still present on the plant ensuring a sufficient yield of approximately two cups.
Rinse the harvested stinging nettle leaves thoroughly with cool running water to remove any dirt debris and then gently pat dry with clean paper towels.

Combine one teaspoon of dried stinging nettle leaves or one tablespoon of fresh nettle leaves with one cup of boiling filtered water in a medium-sized heat-resistant glass container allowing it to steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and then cool the liquid to room temperature within two hours.
Refrigerate the cooled nettle tea for up to 24 hours before consumption drinking one cup twice daily as needed to help alleviate ringworm symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with ringworm because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-fungal compound that inhibits the growth of fungal infections. The active compounds in turmeric work to reduce inflammation and promote healing, allowing the body to naturally fight off the infection. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate the fungus from the skin, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing the appearance of ringworm lesions. Regular consumption of herbal turmeric teas may provide relief and alleviate symptoms associated with this common fungal infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, exhibits antifungal activity against Candida albicans, one of the most common fungi associated with ringworm, helping to combat fungal infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin and other polyphenols, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting healing and tissue repair.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric tea's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can damage skin tissues and hinder the body's natural healing response to ringworm infections, promoting overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to be effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the turmeric plant have been used for centuries to treat skin conditions, including ringworm, due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried turmeric powder and 1 teaspoon of dried ginger powder in a small bowl.
Boil one cup of water in a medium-sized saucepan for exactly 7 minutes.
Add the turmeric and ginger powders to the boiling water while stirring counterclockwise for 30 seconds.
Reduce heat to simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the tea to infuse and reduce slightly in volume.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and let it cool before consumption.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for ringworm are natural remedies made from plants, leaves, and roots that have antifungal properties to combat the fungal infection. They work by reducing inflammation, soothing itching, and promoting healing of the affected skin. Examples of herbal teas effective against ringworm include chamomile, tea tree, and neem teas, which help to alleviate symptoms and promote healthy skin. By using these teas, individuals with ringworm can reduce discomfort, prevent further infection, and improve overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ringworm with herbal teas is to combine several herbs into a concoction that fosters a healthy immune system. A blend of tea tree oil, goldenseal, and calendula has antibacterial and antifungal properties that target the fungal infection. Drink 1-2 cups of this tea mixture three times daily for up to six weeks. Additionally, apply a compress soaked in the tea solution directly to the affected area twice a day.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal teas are fungal infections such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and toenail fungus. Certain herbs like tea tree oil, chamomile, and goldenseal have antifungal properties that help soothe and combat these infections. The teas can be used topically or taken internally to promote healing and reduce symptoms like itching, burning, and discomfort.
